## Priority Gaps

### 1. NEC#1: Data provenance (severity 5/5)
- European Union AI Act: Art. 53.1(b) ÔÇö training data governance and provenance
- China Provisions on Generative AI: Art. 7 ÔÇö training data legality and provenance
- ISO/IEC 42001 AI Management System: 6.1.2 ÔÇö data management for AI lifecycle
- OECD AI Principles: Principle 1.2 ÔÇö transparency of data sources

### 2. NEC#12: Bias assessment (severity 5/5)
- European Union AI Act: Art. 10.2 ÔÇö bias examination and mitigation
- EU General Data Protection Regulation: Art. 22 ÔÇö automated decision-making safeguards
- United States FTC Act: Section 5 ÔÇö discriminatory algorithmic practices
- OECD AI Principles: Principle 1.5 ÔÇö fairness and non-discrimination

### 3. NEC#13: Accountable entity (severity 5/5)
- European Union AI Act: Art. 53.1 preamble ÔÇö provider identification required
- United States FTC Act: Section 5 ÔÇö entity accountability for AI systems
- China Provisions on Generative AI: Art. 5 ÔÇö provider responsibility declaration
- OECD AI Principles: Principle 1.5 ÔÇö accountability

### 4. NEC#2: License attribution (severity 5/5)
- European Union AI Act: Art. 53.1(c) ÔÇö copyright and related rights compliance
- United States DMCA: ┬º512 ÔÇö safe harbor requires proper attribution
- United Kingdom CDPA 1988: s.29A ÔÇö text and data mining exception conditions
- Japan Copyright Act: Art. 30-4 ÔÇö information analysis exception scope

### 5. NEC#6: Safety evaluation (severity 5/5)
- European Union AI Act: Art. 9 ÔÇö risk management system requirements
- China Provisions on Generative AI: Art. 8 ÔÇö safety assessment obligations
- United Kingdom AI Regulation: AI Regulation White Paper ÔÇö safety framework
- OECD AI Principles: Principle 1.4 ÔÇö robustness, security and safety

## Recommendations
- Consider documenting: Data provenance, Safety evaluation, Human oversight. These elements are referenced in multiple regulatory frameworks.
- Additional documentation for: Training methodology, Intended use, Temporal validity and revocation metadata would strengthen compliance coverage across EU AI Act and related frameworks.
- Existing sections on License attribution, Evaluation results, Accountable entity could be expanded with more structured detail (e.g., named references, quantitative data).
